Title: Golden bananas in the field: elevated fruit pro-vitamin A from the expression of a single banana transgene

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting/key control step; paper states early activation of the rate-limiting enzyme in the carotenoid pathway is essential
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream committed precursor for provitamin A carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: pro-vitamin A carotenoids (β-carotene equivalents, mainly α-carotene and β-carotene)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary provitamin A for alleviating vitamin A deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp; also assessed in peel for some lines
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize polyubiquitin1 promoter (constitutive); banana ACC oxidase promoter (fruit-associated); banana expansin1 promoter (late fruit-associated)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously cloned from fruit of a naturally high-PVA Fe’i banana cultivar Asupina
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: When driven by Ubi, increases PVA from earliest fruit stage (S3 onward); when driven by ACO, stronger in later fruit development; when driven by Exp1, mainly late fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants across plant, ratoon, second ratoon and sucker crops; temperature and bunch filling time affected carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases fruit provitamin A levels; Ubi-MtPsy2a produced the best line with average 55.0 μg/g dw β-CE in full green sucker crop and individual plant up to 73.8 μg/g dw β-CE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transgenesis; field trials; HPLC carotenoid profiling; RT-PCR and qRT-PCR of transgene expression; Southern blot copy number analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Few abnormal phenotypes relative to ZmPsy1; only 7 of 85 MtPsy2a-containing lines showed golden leaf and none showed golden bunch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value biofortification gene for banana; single-gene expression under Ubi promoter was sufficient to exceed target PVA levels; Ubi-MtPsy2a and ACO-MtPsy2a constructs were transferred to Uganda for transformation into local cultivars including EAHB
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PaCrtI co-expression was generally unnecessary and often not beneficial for fruit PVA accumulation; trait levels increased across successive vegetative generations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtPsy2a overexpression increased banana fruit provitamin A in field conditions. 2. Constitutive Ubi-driven MtPsy2a gave the highest and earliest carotenoid accumulation during fruit development. 3. Ubi-MtPsy2a line FT324 exceeded the target by a wide margin, averaging 55.0 μg/g dw β-CE in the sucker crop. 4. ACO-driven MtPsy2a also produced above-target lines without the severe ZmPsy1-associated bunch phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting/key control step; paper concludes early activation of the rate-limiting enzyme is essential
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream committed precursor for provitamin A carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: pro-vitamin A carotenoids (β-carotene equivalents, mainly α-carotene and β-carotene)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary provitamin A for alleviating vitamin A deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp; peel hyperaccumulation in ACO-ZmPsy1 lines with golden bunch phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize polyubiquitin1 promoter (constitutive); banana ACC oxidase promoter (fruit-associated); banana expansin1 promoter (late fruit-associated)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: When driven by Ubi, elevates PVA from earliest fruit stage; when driven by Exp1, accumulation mainly late; when driven by ACO, intermediate timing and strong peel-associated phenotype in many lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants across multiple vegetative generations; temperature and longer bunch filling time enhanced carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases fruit provitamin A, with some lines exceeding target levels, but many lines displayed undesirable phenotypes including golden leaf, golden bunch and stunting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transgenesis; field trials; HPLC carotenoid profiling; RT-PCR and qRT-PCR of transgene expression; Southern blot copy number analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High frequency of undesirable phenotypes, especially golden bunch in ACO-ZmPsy1 lines and golden leaf/stunting in some Exp1-ZmPsy1 lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for PVA biofortification but less preferred than MtPsy2a because of frequent abnormal phenotypes; promoter choice is critical to reduce defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ACO-ZmPsy1 lines accumulated extremely high peel β-CE without necessarily increasing pulp beyond MtPsy2a lines; Ubi-ZmPsy1 lines still generated above-target fruit PVA in later generations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPsy1 overexpression increased banana fruit provitamin A under field conditions. 2. Ubi-driven and some ACO-driven ZmPsy1 lines exceeded the target β-CE level. 3. Many ZmPsy1 lines showed undesirable golden leaf, golden bunch, and/or stunting phenotypes. 4. Promoter-dependent timing of expression strongly influenced when carotenoids accumulated during fruit development.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; paper indicates little effect when expressed alone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: more unsaturated carotenoid intermediates leading toward lycopene
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces sequential desaturation steps converting phytoene toward lycopene in the bacterial-type carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediates required for downstream provitamin A carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary provitamin A for alleviating vitamin A deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize polyubiquitin1 promoter (constitutive) or banana expansin1 promoter (late fruit-associated)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in fruit with Exp1 or Ubi promoters, but transgene alone showed little effect on fruit PVA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in multiple crop generations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PaCrtI alone had little effect on fruit provitamin A accumulation and combinations with PSY were generally not superior to PSY alone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transgenesis; field trial fruit carotenoid analysis by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major phenotype effect reported; presence did not appear to affect phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low standalone value in this banana system; PSY-only strategies, especially MtPsy2a, are more effective and simpler
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-transgene PaCrtI lines were not progressed further because they showed little effect on PVA levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaCrtI alone did not substantially raise banana fruit provitamin A. 2. Co-expression with PSY was usually not better than PSY alone. 3. The study deprioritized PaCrtI-only lines for further analysis.
